title: Chemical Reactions and Balancing Equations
subject: Science
learningOutcomes: Apply the Law of Conservation of Mass to balance chemical equations.
Observe real-time reactant vs product atom count tally meters.

objectives.text: Set coefficient $a=2, b=1, c=2$ to balance $2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$.
Verify that Hydrogen (4 atoms) and Oxygen (2 atoms) match on both sides.

theory: A chemical equation must have an equal number of atoms of each element on both sides to satisfy the Law of Conservation of Mass.
hints: Use the coefficient stepper buttons (+) and (-) to adjust stoichiometric multipliers.
Watch the status badge turn GREEN when the equation is balanced.
observationPrompts: Why can coefficients be changed when balancing an equation, but subscripts in chemical formulas cannot?

realWorldExamples.title: Industrial Hydrogen Combustion
realWorldExamples.description: Rocket propulsion uses stoichiometric hydrogen-oxygen combustion ($2\text{H}_2 + \text{O}_2 \rightarrow 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$) for maximum thrust.

commonMistakes: Changing chemical subscripts (e.g. turning H₂O into H₂O₂) instead of modifying coefficients.
